events.description: George Peters wrote a letter to Senator Murray regarding patent in fee
Senator Murray prepared and introduced new bill in Congress to authorize issuance of patent in fee to George Peters
S. 716 introduced by Senator Murray, read twice and referred to Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
Department of the Interior issued report on S. 716, recommending enactment with amendments, signed by Undersecretary Oscar L. Chapman
Joseph C. O'Mahoney sent letter to Senator Murray attaching Department of Interior report on S. 716 and requesting advice on amendments
House Committee on Public Lands reported S. 716 favorably without amendment, committed to Committee of the Whole House and ordered to be printed
Senator Murray sent letter to George Peters advising that S. 716 passed the Senate on April 11th and was reported favorably out of House Committee on Public Lands, enclosing copy of House report
S. 716 passed the House and cleared for President's signature
Senator Murray sent telegram to George Peters advising that S. 716 passed the House and cleared for President's signature
George Peters indicated desire to sell his land to George Redfield, another Crow Indian
Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs amended S. 716 at suggestion of Department of the Interior to permit Secretary to sell land under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee

financial_transactions.description: Proposed sale of 840 acres of George Peters' allotted land on Crow Indian Reservation to another Crow Indian under existing regulations, authorized by Secretary of the Interior

fee_patents.buyer: unnamed Crow Indian (originally George Redfield mentioned as desired buyer in 1921)

correspondence.subject: Request regarding patent in fee for allotted land
correspondence.action_requested: Introduction of bill to authorize issuance of patent in fee
correspondence.outcome: Senator Murray introduced S. 716 on January 31, 1949

correspondence.subject: Response to Peters' letter of January 20th; introduction of new bill
correspondence.outcome: Peters informed that Murray introduced new bill and would do utmost to get it passed

correspondence.recipient_title: Chairman, Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
correspondence.subject: Department of Interior report on S. 716, authorizing issuance of patent in fee to George Peters
correspondence.action_requested: Amendment of S. 716 to authorize Secretary to sell land to a Crow Indian under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee; amendment of title accordingly
correspondence.outcome: Report forwarded to Senator Murray by O'Mahoney on March 10, 1949; bill subsequently amended as suggested

correspondence.sender_title: Chairman, Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
correspondence.subject: Attached Department of Interior report on S. 716; request for advice on proposed amendments
correspondence.action_requested: Murray to advise whether amendments suggested by Department of Interior are agreeable
correspondence.outcome: Bill was amended as suggested by Department of Interior

correspondence.subject: S. 716 passed Senate on April 11th and reported favorably by House Committee on Public Lands
correspondence.action_requested: None; informational with enclosed copy of House report
correspondence.outcome: Peters informed of bill's progress; bill subsequently passed House on May 17, 1949

correspondence.subject: S. 716 passed House and cleared for President's signature
correspondence.action_requested: None; informational telegram via Western Union Day Letter
correspondence.outcome: Peters notified bill passed House on May 17, 1949 and cleared for President's signature

legislative_actions.committee: Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
legislative_actions.outcome: Read twice and referred to Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs

legislative_actions.committee: Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
legislative_actions.outcome: Bill amended at suggestion of Department of Interior to authorize Secretary to sell land under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee; title amended accordingly
Reported favorably without amendment; committed to Committee of the Whole House and ordered to be printed; Report No. 508, 81st Congress, 1st Session
Passed the House and cleared for President's signature
